zh.lifeSummary: Maud Matilda Marshall是Sir William Marshall和Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare的女兒，於1192年出生在英國威爾斯布洛可爾布洛可。她於大約1206年與Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1248年3月27日在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾特 艾比享年56歲，埋葬在威爾士蒙姆爾特特 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Maud Matilda Marshall是Sir William Marshall和Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare的女儿，于1192年出生在英国威尔斯布洛可爾布洛可。她于大约1206年与Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1248年3月27日在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾特 艾比享年56岁，埋葬在威尔士蒙姆爾特特 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Marshall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.
Americanized form of German   Marschall  .
Americanized form of French   Mercier  .

variants.name: Marchel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jacek, Ryszard.  German Ernst.

Polish: probably from French maréchal or Old French maresc(h)al, which was originally an occupational name for someone who was in charge of the horses of a household, later a military rank for a senior general. See Marshall and compare Marszalek .

variants.name: Mercier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Gaston, Lucien, Aurele, Jacques, Normand, Laurent, Michel, Valmont.

French and Channel Islands (Jersey): occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, from Late Latin merciarius (an agent derivative of merx, genitive mercis ‘merchandise’). This name was also taken to England by Huguenot refugees. Compare Marshall and Mercy .

variants.name: Maniscalco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Vito, Sal, Antonio, Ignazio, Carmelo, Francesco, Gaspare, Saverio, Antonino, Carlo, Damiano.

Italian (southern): occupational name from maniscalco ‘farrier’, altered from marescalco by folk etymology, probably influenced by mano ‘hand’, from medieval Latin mariscalcus, marescalcus. Compare Marshall .

variants.name: Marchal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Jacques, Maryse, Philippe, Pierre, Veronique.

French and Walloon: status name or occupational name from Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The term is of ancient Germanic origin (from marah ‘horse, mare’ + scalc ‘servant’) and was originally applied to a man who looked after horses. In the Middle Ages it also came to be used on the one hand as an occupational name for a farrier, and on the other as a status name for an officer of state, in particular a member of a royal household with military responsibilities. Compare English Marshall and 2 below.
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland, and is found mainly in Glamorgan, Wales.

variants.name: Maskell
meanings: English: variant of Marshall , derived from Middle English, Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The first element in the placename Mascallsbury (Essex) derives from the surname of a 13th-century individual named Robert le Marescal.

variants.name: Marszalek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Piotr, Alicja, Andrzej, Eugeniusz, Henryk, Jerzy, Kazimierz, Mieczyslaw, Wieslaw, Zbigniew, Zofia, Zygmunt.

Polish (Marszałek): occupational or status name from the vocabulary word marszałek ‘marshal’, denoting anything from a high court official, a field marshal, or the chairman of the Polish parliament, to the senior servant in a great household. Compare English Marshall .

variants.name: Marshell
meanings: English: variant of   Marshall  .

variants.name: Marschall
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Hedwig, Juergen, Reinhard, Theresia, Wilfried.

German: occupational name for a man in the service of a member of the nobility, originally one who looked after the horses, Middle High German marschalc (from mar(c) ‘noble horse’ + schalk ‘servant’). In time the term came to be used for a military rank or a certain court official. Compare Marshall .
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marshal
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Marshall  .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: Maud Matilda Marshall was born in 1192, in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom as the daughter of Sir William Marshall and Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare. She married Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk about 1206. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 27 March 1248, in Tintern Abbey, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 56, and was buried in Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, Wales.

fr.lifeSummary: Maud Matilda Marshall est née en 1192, à Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, fille de Sir William Marshall et Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare. Elle a épousé Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk environ 1206. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 27 mars 1248, à Tintern Abbey, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterrée à Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, Galles.

ko.lifeSummary: Maud Matilda Marshall(은)는 1192년 영국 웨일스 p브로케실르 p브로크에서 Sir William Marshall(와)과 Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1206년 Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1248년 3월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 틴터 애브브이에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스 몬모우스실르 틴터 틴터 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Maud Matilda Marshall nasceu em 1192, em Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales, United Kingdom filha de Sir William Marshall e Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare. Ela casou-se com Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk aproximadamente 1206. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 27 de março de 1248, em Tintern Abbey, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 56 anos, e foi sepultada em Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, Gales.

ru.Thetford St Mary the Less, Suffolk, Engl: тетфорд ст мэри т лесс, суффолк, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Maud Matilda Marshall родилась в 1192 г. в местности пемброк, пемброкешир, Уэльс, Великобритания. Её родителей звали Sir William Marshall и Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk около 1206 г. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 27 марта 1248 г. в местности тинтирн эбби, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 56 лет и была похоронена в местности тинтирн эбби, тинтирн, монмоутшир, Уэльс.

es.Thetford St Mary the Less, Suffolk, Engl: Thetford St Mary the Less, Suffolk, Inglaterra
es.Tintern Abbey, Gloucestershire, England,: Tintern Abbey, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Maud Matilda Marshall nació en 1192, en Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales, Reino Unido como hija de Sir William Marshall y Countess Of Pembroke Isabel De Clare. Se casó con Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk aproximadamente 1206. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 27 de marzo de 1248, en Tintern Abbey, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultada en Tintern Abbey, Tintern, Monmouthshire, Gales.
